mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with a declining federal funds rate indicates robust consumer spending supported by cheap credit, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction (Rank IC < -0.02) in post-shock environments.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01), or positive GDP prints coincide with negative equity returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 205.0, persistent inflationary pressure forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.0, or the threshold effect vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) in high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms broad-based economic resilience and rising household wealth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate during periods of historically tight labor markets signals a sudden labor market correction, triggering recession fears and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day returns when the labor market is historically tight, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 in this specific regime.
